config.title: Berlin Night Transport — FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists getting around Berlin late at night.

items.q: Does Berlin public transport run all night?
items.a: On Friday-to-Saturday and Saturday-to-Sunday nights, U-Bahn and S-Bahn trains normally run through the night. On Sunday-to-Thursday nights, U-Bahn and S-Bahn usually stop around 1:00 to 1:30 AM, and night buses plus Metrotrams cover the gap.

items.q: How do Berlin night buses work?
items.a: Berlin night buses are marked with an N before the route number. The N1, N2, N3, N5, N6, N7, N8 and N9 broadly follow the corresponding U-Bahn routes during the weekday-night gap.

items.q: Can I use a normal Berlin ticket at night?
items.a: Yes. A valid Berlin public transport ticket works across S-Bahn, U-Bahn, tram, bus and ferry inside the fare zones you bought. The night service is not a separate ticket system.

items.q: What is the safest way to get back to a hotel late in Berlin?
items.a: Plan the route before you leave, check the current BVG or S-Bahn app, use well-lit main stops when possible, and save a taxi or ride-hail fallback if the next night bus connection is awkward.

items.q: Is a taxi better than night transport in Berlin?
items.a: Use night transport first when the route is simple, especially on weekend nights. A taxi or ride-hail makes more sense if the app shows long waits, multiple late transfers, heavy luggage, or a very tired group.

items.q: Can I use Berlin night transport after the BerlinWalk tour?
items.a: Usually yes, although the BerlinWalk route ends much earlier than the night network becomes relevant. The tour lasts about **2 hours** and ends near Hackescher Markt, where S-Bahn, tram and bus options are close by.
